title: cerastium octandrum var. scandens
intro: Cerastium octandrum var. scandens, also known as creeping chickweed, is a low-growing, spreading plant belonging to the carnation family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihistaminic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and alleviate allergy symptoms. The health benefits of creeping chickweed include its ability to relieve pain, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, creeping chickweed is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
